articles.title: Violence in Australian town after arrest of man over girl's murder
articles.summary: Unrest took place outside hospital where man suspected of murdering a five-year-old girl was being treated.

articles.title: Myanmar ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi moved to house arrest, military says
articles.summary: The Nobel Peace Prize laureate has been in detention since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.

articles.summary: It follows growing tensions over the return of Russia for the first time since the full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

articles.title: China scraps tariffs for all but one African nation

articles.title: Deadly Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon despite ceasefire
articles.summary: At least nine people - including two children - were killed, according to Lebanon's health ministry.

articles.title: Trainee driver crashes bus into River Seine
articles.summary: The driver hit a parked car and veered off the road into the river - about 12 miles south of Paris - early on Thursday.

articles.title: Belgium plans to nationalise nuclear power plants
articles.summary: Prime Minister Bart De Wever said the move would see "less dependence on fossil fuel imports and more control over our own supply".

articles.title: Trump signs bill to end record shutdown over immigration enforcement
articles.summary: The shutdown caused chaos in airports across the US as politicians feuded over funds for Trump's immigration crackdown.
